key_highlights: Iran war and naval blockade continue to spike oil prices to 4-year highs, with US gas prices hitting $4.39/gallon and inflation surging.
Federal Reserve holds rates steady amid war-driven inflation, with some officials signaling next move could be a hike.
Big Tech earnings (Apple, Alphabet, Amazon) beat estimates, but Meta plunges; AI spending remains central theme.
US debt tops 100% of GDP, adding fiscal pressure as war costs mount.
Gold rises on Iran peace hopes, while oil falls on same news; markets mixed but Dow climbs on Apple and Caterpillar strength.
Moderna beats revenue estimates on COVID vaccine sales; Exxon and Chevron beat profit estimates on oil rally.
China EV makers (BYD, Geely, Xiaomi) see sales bumps; Nissan drops US EV production plan.
Japan intervenes with $34.5B to support yen; bond traders eye UK gilt selloff ahead of local elections.
Labor unrest threatens South Korea manufacturing; Colombia central bank surprises with steepener bets.
Cathie Wood buys beaten-down AI stock; SoftBank-linked deal raises nearly $1B for US data centers.

sectors.trend: Oil prices at 4-year highs due to Iran war and naval blockade; Goldman Sachs raises oil price forecast. Exxon and Chevron beat profit estimates. Natural gas futures fall alongside oil price drop. BP reviews UK North Sea assets.
sectors.implications: Energy sector profits surge, but higher oil prices risk recession and inflation. Investors should consider energy stocks for short-term gains but watch for peace-driven pullbacks. Diversification into renewables and LNG may hedge geopolitical risk.

sectors.trend: Big Tech earnings mixed: Apple, Alphabet, Amazon beat; Meta plunges. AI spending remains central, with SoftBank-linked data center deal, Oracle supply agreement, and ASML as key AI stock. Cathie Wood buys beaten-down AI stock. Snowflake partners with Appian.
sectors.implications: AI infrastructure spending continues to drive growth, but valuation concerns persist. Focus on companies with strong AI monetization (NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL) and data center plays (ORCL, SNOW). Meta's selloff may present entry point if AI strategy solidifies.

sectors.trend: China EV makers (BYD, Geely, Xiaomi, Leapmotor) report strong April sales. Nissan drops US EV production plan. Rivian reports smaller Q1 loss, reaffirms outlook. Tesla rivals gain market share.
sectors.implications: Chinese EV makers continue to dominate, while legacy automakers struggle with EV transition. US-China tensions (ban on Chinese vehicles) may reshape supply chains. Rivian's DOE loan progress is positive. Investors should favor Chinese EV leaders and US players with strong execution.

sectors.trend: Bank of America resets Microsoft forecast; JPMorgan executive denies allegations; AIG shares rise on profit jump; Blue Owl defends bear thesis; BNY Mellon continues bull run. SEC semiannual reporting proposal clears White House review.
sectors.implications: Financial sector remains resilient, with strong earnings from AIG and BNY Mellon. Regulatory changes (SEC reporting) may increase compliance costs. JPMorgan legal case is isolated but worth monitoring. Diversified exposure to large-cap banks and insurers recommended.

sectors.trend: Gold and silver rise on Iran peace hopes; copper costs turn negative for miners as gold/silver byproducts soar. Aluminum gains on naval blockade. Oil falls on peace hopes but remains elevated.
sectors.implications: Gold is a safe haven amid geopolitical uncertainty and inflation. Copper weakness may signal industrial demand slowdown. Aluminum benefits from supply disruptions. Investors should hold gold as hedge, but be cautious on base metals.

sectors.trend: Moderna beats revenue estimates on COVID vaccine sales; Lilly raises full-year guidance on new medicines. Estée Lauder reports higher Q3 sales.
sectors.implications: Healthcare sector shows resilience with strong earnings from Moderna and Lilly. COVID vaccine demand remains, but focus shifts to new product pipelines. Consumer health (Estée Lauder) benefits from premium demand. Defensive positioning in healthcare recommended.

sectors.trend: Miami's biggest boom ever per billionaire Perez; Blue Cross Blue Shield settlement payments; Thermos recall (consumer goods).
sectors.implications: Real estate in select markets (Miami) continues to boom, but higher mortgage rates (due to oil spike) may cool demand. Infrastructure spending (data centers) remains strong. Investors should focus on data center REITs and Sun Belt markets.

key_themes: Iran war and geopolitical risk dominate markets, driving oil, gold, and inflation.
AI spending continues to be the primary growth driver for tech, with data center buildout accelerating.
Central banks (Fed, ECB, BOJ) remain cautious, with Fed holding rates and Japan intervening in FX.
US fiscal concerns mount as debt tops 100% of GDP, exacerbated by war costs.
China EV makers surge while legacy automakers retreat; US-China trade tensions escalate.
Inflation pressures intensify due to energy prices, impacting consumer spending and Fed policy.
Labor unrest in South Korea and UK retail administration highlight global economic fragility.
M&A activity continues (Paramount lawsuit, Versant sale, SoftBank data center deal).

risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase cash and gold holdings; reduce exposure to oil-sensitive sectors; hedge with energy stocks and commodities.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Inflation surge and Fed policy uncertainty
risk_assessment.mitigation: Favor TIPS, short-duration bonds, and value stocks; avoid long-duration fixed income. Monitor Fed comments for rate hike signals.
Diversify into non-US assets (emerging markets, European equities); hold gold and cryptocurrencies as hedges.
Take profits on high-flying AI stocks; rotate into value and dividend-paying tech. Focus on companies with strong cash flows (MSFT, AAPL).

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Supply chain disruptions (Iran war, US-China tensions)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Invest in onshoring beneficiaries (manufacturing, semiconductors); avoid companies with heavy Iran or China exposure. Monitor shipping and grain flows.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Labor unrest and economic slowdown (South Korea, UK)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce exposure to Korean and UK equities; favor US and Japan markets. Monitor wage inflation and strike impacts.
Hedge FX exposure using futures or ETFs; favor USD-denominated assets. Avoid speculative FX trades.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy energy stocks on pullbacks (XOM, CVX, COP)
opportunities.rationale: Oil prices remain elevated due to Iran war; Exxon and Chevron beat profit estimates. Goldman Sachs raised oil forecast. Peace hopes may cause short-term dips, but structural supply tightness supports prices.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate AI infrastructure plays (NVDA, ORCL, SNOW, ASML)
opportunities.rationale: AI spending remains robust with data center buildout (SoftBank deal, Oracle supply agreement). ASML is key AI stock. Cathie Wood buying beaten-down AI stocks signals value.
Gold rises on Iran peace hopes but remains a safe haven amid inflation and geopolitical risk. Central bank buying and fiscal concerns support prices.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in Chinese EV leaders (BYDDF, LI, XPEV)
opportunities.rationale: BYD, Geely, Xiaomi, Leapmotor report strong April sales. US-China tensions may create buying opportunities. Chinese EV makers are gaining global market share.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Apple (AAPL) on strong earnings and China sales
opportunities.rationale: Apple beat Q2 estimates with strong iPhone and China sales. Stock rose on earnings. Defensive quality with AI integration potential.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider healthcare defensive plays (MRNA, LLY, EL)
opportunities.rationale: Moderna beat revenue estimates; Lilly raised guidance; Estée Lauder reported higher sales. Healthcare offers stability amid volatility.

defensive_moves.rationale: Geopolitical uncertainty, inflation, and Fed policy uncertainty warrant higher cash reserves. Money market rates up to 4.01% APY provide yield.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge with put options on S&P 500 (SPY)
defensive_moves.rationale: Market sentiment is neutral but fragile; Iran war escalation or Fed hawkishness could trigger selloff. Puts provide downside protection.
Inflation spike and potential Fed rate hike make long-duration bonds risky. Favor short-duration TIPS or floating rate notes.
US fiscal concerns and geopolitical risks favor international diversification. Emerging assets extend gains on Iran peace hopes. Japan intervention supports yen.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid Meta (META) until AI strategy clarifies
defensive_moves.rationale: Meta plunged after earnings; AI spending concerns and competition from Alphabet/Amazon. Wait for clearer monetization path.

market_outlook.short_term: Markets will remain volatile due to Iran war developments, inflation data, and Fed policy signals. Oil prices may stay elevated but could drop sharply on peace deal. Tech earnings divergence (Apple up, Meta down) will drive sector rotation. Gold and energy likely to outperform. US dollar may strengthen on safe-haven flows.
market_outlook.long_term: AI infrastructure spending will continue to drive tech growth, but valuations may compress if inflation persists. US fiscal trajectory is concerning but manageable in near term. China EV makers will gain global share. Geopolitical risks (Iran, US-China) will reshape supply chains. Central banks will remain cautious, with potential rate cuts in 2027 if inflation eases. Gold and digital assets (Bitcoin) may benefit from debasement fears.
key_catalysts: Iran-US peace negotiations and naval blockade status
Fed rate decision and inflation data (CPI, PCE)
Big Tech earnings (remaining reports from Amazon, Alphabet, etc.)
US debt ceiling and fiscal policy developments
China EV sales data and US tariff decisions
Japan FX intervention and yen stability
Oil price trajectory and OPEC+ response
Labor market data and wage inflation
Global supply chain disruptions (Iran, Red Sea)
watch_list: Oil prices (WTI, Brent) and gas prices at pump
US 10-year Treasury yield and yield curve
Iran war headlines and diplomatic developments
UK gilt yields and local election outcomes
